description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the node:latest Docker image and caches the node_modules directory with a key of $CI_BUILD_REF_NAME, including untracked files. Define three stages: verify, test, and publish, and add a before_script that sets a VERSION variable to the tag or branch name and echoes it. In the verify stage add a codestyle job that runs "npm ci" then "npm test". In the test stage add a node:latest job that runs "npm ci" then "npm run unit" and records coverage using the regex /^All files[^|]*\|[^|]*\s+([\d\.]+)/. In the publish stage add an npm job that runs only on tags matching ^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+(-rc\.\d+|)$, is manual, sets VERSION_TAG to "next" if the tag contains "-rc" otherwise "latest", writes the NPM auth token to .npmrc, runs "npm ci" and "npm publish --tag $VERSION_TAG", and sets the environment named npm with the URL https://www.npmjs.com/package/${CI_PROJECT_NAME}.
